summary.intent: Address nine Codex review threads on PR #727 (fogodev/ars-ui), push fixes, and then resolve a CI Coverage/CRAP regression that appeared in the subsequent CI run.
summary.outcome: All nine Codex threads were replied to and resolved with commit b696c490. A follow-up commit fixed two CRAP complexity regressions (InputType::as_str in ars-components and apply_attr_value in ars-dom) that were blocking the Coverage CI gate. A transient network failure in Error Variant Coverage remained unresolved at session end.

repo: The repo uses a `cargo crap` gate with a `.crap-baseline.json` file and `--fail-regression` flag; CRAP score increases in changed or path-shifted functions block CI.
CI LCOV artifacts use `/home/runner/work/ars-ui/ars-ui/` paths; local replay requires `SF:` path normalization to the local checkout root.
The `cargo xclippy` and `cargo xfmt` commands are the required pre-push lint/format gates for this repo.
Adapter scaffolding should create `src/<category>/<component>/mod.rs` (directory-backed layout), not flat files.
Widget, fixture, and E2E harness category lookup must come from `spec/manifest.toml` metadata, not a hard-coded component map.
Leptos and Dioxus input E2E fixtures must inject CSS Checkbox STYLES before rendering styled fixtures.
Dioxus widget public CSS include targets are tracked as public symlinks to crate companion styles, verified by an xtask regression test.
All six widget form demos must set controlled terms checkbox `default_checked` to `Checked` so reset restores expected checked state.
Adapter presence checks require parsed `#[test]`/`#[wasm_bindgen_test]` attributes; empty or comment-only files are rejected.
Sketch validation must track final matrix data-row count and reject header-only placeholder matrices.
Workflow delivery checks in adapter-parity must scope from usage docs or counterpart sketches so missing usage docs cause failures instead of silent drops.
The duplicate-helper regex must match `const`, `async`, `unsafe`, and `pub(crate)`-style private helper functions.

code.finding: InputType::as_str was a wide 23-arm match with partial coverage, causing CRAP regression. Fixed by switching to a static indexed token table with a test covering every HTML token. Explicit discriminants were added to prevent future variant ordering shifts.

apply_attr_value mixed AttrValue conversion with DOM mutation in one function, inflating cyclomatic complexity past the CRAP gate. Refactored to separate value conversion from set/remove dispatch. This code is behind a wasm/web cfg and must be checked with --target wasm32-unknown-unknown.

workflow: GitHub blocks `gh run rerun --failed` while the workflow is still in progress; must wait for all jobs to complete before rerunning failed jobs.
Saving CI job logs locally with `gh api .../logs > /tmp/file.log` and stripping ANSI color with `perl -pe 's/\e\[[0-9;]*m//g'` is necessary to reliably parse cargo-crap table output.
The cargo-crap CLI's table format prints thousands of unchanged/removed rows; filtering for `┆ +N` delta columns is needed to isolate actual regressions.
Transient CI failures (e.g., curl HTTP2 framing errors during crate registry downloads) must be distinguished from real code failures before deciding to rerun vs. fix.
When CI LCOV paths don't match the local checkout, use `sed` to normalize `SF:` lines before running local coverage tools.
Codex review is triggered by posting `@codex review` as a PR comment via `gh pr comment`; stale 👀 reactions can indicate a stalled review that may need re-triggering.
Resolving GitHub review threads requires both posting a reply comment (`gh api -X POST .../comments` with `in_reply_to`) and calling the `resolveReviewThread` GraphQL mutation separately.

friction: GitHub's `gh run rerun --failed` command refuses to run while the workflow is still active, requiring repeated polling loops before the rerun could be attempted.
The cargo-crap terminal output is extremely noisy (thousands of unchanged/removed rows) and embeds ANSI color codes, making it difficult to identify actual regressions from raw log output.
CI LCOV artifacts reference runner-absolute paths, breaking local cargo-crap replay without a manual sed normalization step.
The Coverage CI job took an unusually long time (multiple polling cycles), and its failure was buried in a large log requiring manual line-range extraction.
A GraphQL query typo in one polling iteration (`reviewThreads` closing brace misplaced) caused a failed query that had to be corrected in the next cycle.
The pre-commit hook ran silently for an extended period with no output, making it unclear whether it was progressing or hung.
The transient `Error Variant Coverage` network failure (curl HTTP2 framing error on pin-project-lite) could not be rerun during the session due to the active workflow restriction.
open_items: The transient `Error Variant Coverage` CI job failure (curl HTTP2 framing error fetching pin-project-lite) was never rerun; it needs a manual `gh run rerun --failed` once CI settles.
The `ars-dom::apply_attr_value` CRAP delta shown in local replay against CI LCOV may still appear in CI if baseline path alignment differs; should be verified in the next CI run.
Codex had 👀 for an extended period with no new threads posted; if the next CI run triggers another Codex review cycle, watch for stale review state requiring a fresh `@codex review` trigger.
The `.crap-baseline.json` was not updated in this session; if the refactored functions now have lower CRAP scores, the baseline should be updated to lock in the improvement.
